HERB CRUSTED LAMB CHOPS

8 lamb chops (2-inch thickness)
3 tablespoons fresh rosemary, finely chopped
2 tablespoons fresh thyme leaves
4 cloves garlic, minced
¼ cup bread crumbs (panko preferred)
2 tablespoons grated Parmigiano
2 tablespoons Dijon mustard
3 tablespoons olive oil
Salt and cracked pepper
Juice of 1 lemon
2 lemons, cut into wedges

Method:
1. Pat lamb chops completely dry with paper towels
2. Season both sides generously with salt and pepper
3. Mix rosemary, thyme, garlic, breadcrumbs, and Parm together
4. Brush each chop with thin layer of Dijon mustard
5. Press herb mixture firmly onto both sides of each chop
6. Let rest at room temperature for 20 minutes
7. Heat olive oil in large cast iron skillet over medium-high heat
8. When oil is smoking slightly, carefully add chops
9. Sear 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare (internal temp 130-135°F)
10. Remove to warm plate and rest 5 minutes
11. Drizzle with lemon juice
12. Serve with fresh lemon wedges and roasted root vegetables
13. Pairs beautifully with a full-bodied red wine

Note: Do not overcrowd the pan. If necessary, sear in 2 batches.
